name: homepage_0_home

observed_strengths.observation: H1 'Fuel for Mind & Body' — clear functional benefit positioning that differentiates from standard coffee (energy without jitters framing) and immediately communicates the wellness value proposition
OG description confirms '60,000 5-star reviews' — massive social proof claim present in meta, indicating a large verified review base even if not surfaced as a visible badge in headings

Product catalog organized by functional benefit families in homepage: Energy & Focus Blends (Coffee, Original, Matcha, Turmeric), Protein Blends (Chocolate, Vanilla), Calm Blends (Rest, Hot Cacao) — benefit-first navigation lowers decision friction
'ApprovedBy Leaders' H2 — influencer/authority endorsement section visible above fold, adding social proof beyond reviews
Active promotional calendar: 'Shop Summer sale - up to 43% off' + 'NEW - Try Our Limited Time Strawberry Matcha' — dual urgency signals (seasonal sale + limited edition launch) running concurrently
Subscription login at /tools/recurring/get-subscription-access in nav — subscription management directly accessible from all pages, reinforcing retention-first positioning

missing_or_weak.observation: 60,000 5-star reviews claim exists only in meta/OG tags — no visible review count badge or 'stars' H2/H3 on homepage headings detected, meaning this social proof may not be visually surfaced to first-time visitors scanning the page

No email capture heading visible — unlike deathwishcoffee which had '20% OFF YOUR FIRST ORDER' email capture on multiple pages including homepage, no email capture section heading detected on mudwtr homepage

Where-to-buy link not confirmed on homepage — 'Find in Stores' CTA appears on other pages but homepage CTAs don't include a retail/wholesale discovery path for visitors who cannot or prefer not to buy online

purchase_path_notes: Homepage has strong benefit framing and product family organization. The absence of a visible reviews section heading on the page (vs. the meta 60k claim) is the primary social proof gap. CTAs are promotional and product-linked — no discount email capture as homepage entry mechanism.
